question: What is unique_key in a dbt incremental model and what does it control?
answer: unique_key tells dbt which column(s) uniquely identify a row. When set, dbt uses MERGE (or delete+insert) on incremental runs: rows matching the key are updated, new rows are inserted. Without unique_key, dbt simply appends all new rows with no deduplication, which risks duplicates if the same record appears in two runs. Set unique_key to your natural or surrogate key for any model where upstream data can be updated or corrected.
